headline: MRT3 Circle Line: Budget 2023 Targets ~RM45B Total, Down from RM68B in 2018
context: MRT Corp received final railway scheme approval on 17 July 2025. Budget 2023 set a target to bring MRT3 total cost under RM45 billion (about RM31B construction + RM6-7B land), down from the 2018 RM68B estimate. The project was scrapped May 2018 and re-approved March 2022.

cards.big: Budget 2023 targeted bringing MRT3 total cost under RM45 billion, from the 2018 RM68 billion estimate. Final railway scheme approval landed 17 July 2025.
cards.sub: Construction allocation about RM31 billion plus RM6-7 billion for land acquisition. The figures are a target, not a binding statutory cap.

cards.big: The project was scrapped in May 2018, then approved again in March 2022 with MRT Corp appointed as project owner.
cards.sub: Land acquisition is targeted for completion in 2026; construction is expected from 2027.

cards.big: About 51 km orbital alignment with 10 interchange stations integrated with existing MRT, LRT, KTM and Monorail lines.
cards.sub: Designed to close the Klang Valley rail loop. Secondary reporting cites a 2032 operational target.

cards.big: ECRL remains the larger ringgit-cost single rail project at RM74.96 billion total per MOT/MRL figures. MRT3 sits below ECRL but above other current rail builds.
cards.sub: TI-Malaysia called for an Independent Expert Monitor and integrity pact before MRT3 tender documents are finalised.

cards.big: The story is not 'budget ballooning'. The open question is whether the Budget 2023 target translates into actual construction cost discipline.
Reducing the headline target does not eliminate cost-overrun risk. The test is whether construction from 2027 onwards stays close to the RM45 billion target.
